claim: Every single thing you add to an agent risks making it worse; frontier models are so capable that less instruction and shorter system prompts lead to better performance
actionable: Start with the minimal system prompt that achieves your baseline, then only add instructions if testing shows performance improvement
source_title: Don't Build Slop (4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity) - Ara Khan, Cline

claim: Agents are state machines with recursive while loops and conditional end states; visualize the state machine as a mental model to understand agent behavior at any point
actionable: Draw out your agent's state machine (states + transitions) before coding; use it as a reference when debugging unexpected behavior
source_title: Don't Build Slop (4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity) - Ara Khan, Cline

claim: Four levels of agent maturity: (1) use frameworks for prototyping, (2) build your own agents as state machines with strict rules, (3) expose via Kanban UX to manage parallel inference-bound agents, (4) deploy to cloud for scalability
actionable: Progress through these levels based on your needs; start at level 1 for MVP, move to level 2-4 as you productionize
source_title: Don't Build Slop (4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity) - Ara Khan, Cline

claim: Build agents as part of a pseudo-RL pipeline: create a CLI/CI-CD that makes testing and iterating easy, so other agents can modify and test your agent automatically
actionable: Write an agents.md or agents.toml with instructions; set up CI/CD that allows agents to build, test, and iterate on your agent without manual intervention
source_title: Don't Build Slop (4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity) - Ara Khan, Cline

claim: Kanban boards are the ideal UX for managing parallel inference-bound agents; they allow you to visualize all running agents, manage state transitions, and handle agent-to-agent coordination
actionable: Implement a Kanban interface for your agent orchestration where columns represent states and cards represent running agent tasks
source_title: Don't Build Slop (4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity) - Ara Khan, Cline

claim: Frontier model APIs have asymmetries in how they handle reasoning traces and cache; using APIs incorrectly leads to degraded performance with no visibility into the loss
actionable: When upgrading to a new frontier model API, carefully study the spec for reasoning traces, caching, and token formatting; test empirically to ensure API usage is correct
source_title: Don't Build Slop (4 Levels of AI Agent Maturity) - Ara Khan, Cline
